text.title: mcp-transport-stdio-vs-http Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mcp-transport-stdio-vs-http as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running stdio in production multi-user deployment ("we will spawn one stdio process per request").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Process-per-request defeats connection reuse, breaks OAuth flows, and saturates fork()/spawn() under load. Use HTTP.

failure-mode.description: Greenfield server that supports SSE "for compatibility" without naming a single client that needs it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: SSE is transitional. Without a named legacy client and a sunset date, you are paying maintenance tax on dead code.

failure-mode.description: Server uses stdio but is wrapped by a TCP-to-stdio bridge to "make it remote".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The bridge becomes a single-point bottleneck with no auth, no resumability, and no per-tenant isolation. Use Streamable HTTP natively instead.

failure-mode.description: Streamable HTTP server keeps session state in a Python dict (in-process).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Two replicas behind an LB cannot see each other's sessions; the next request hits the wrong replica and the agent loses context mid-loop.
